gui.guide_book.cover_subtitle: Introduction to city building and management
gui.guide_book.open_hint: Left or right click to open the book
gui.guide_book.left_1: 1. Place the city core and finish creating your city.
gui.guide_book.left_2: 2. Use the build box to choose a blueprint and plan the area.
gui.guide_book.left_3: 3. Hire NPCs to begin construction and production.
gui.guide_book.right_1: 1. Logistics nodes connect warehouses and containers together.
gui.guide_book.right_2: 2. The farmland box manages crop selection and farmer tasks.
gui.guide_book.right_3: 3. Control boxes run residential, commercial and industrial flows.
gui.guide_book.hint: Right-click this item at any time to reopen the guide.
gui.guide_book.directory.city: 1. City Page: learn the basics of the city core, build box and early city growth.
gui.guide_book.directory.commerce: 2. Commerce Page: check commercial control boxes, shop operation and money flow.
gui.guide_book.directory.logistics: 3. Logistics Page: review the logistics server, client and network connection tips.
gui.guide_book.directory.hint: Click the tabs on the right to jump directly to a topic page.
gui.guide_book.city.left_1: 1. The city core is the starting point of the whole city system, so place it first before expanding.
gui.guide_book.city.left_2: 2. The build box handles blueprint selection, facing direction and construction area planning.
gui.guide_book.city.left_3: 3. Stabilizing housing, citizens and basic utility boxes early makes later growth much smoother.
gui.guide_book.city.right_1: 1. Clear the construction zone before building to reduce rework and placement issues.
gui.guide_book.city.right_2: 2. Funds, population and free space decide how fast your city can expand.
gui.guide_book.city.right_3: 3. Get the core loop running first, then connect commerce and logistics step by step.
gui.guide_book.commerce.left_1: 1. Commercial control boxes handle daily shop operation and are a major source of city income.
gui.guide_book.commerce.left_2: 2. Different shops sell different goods, and stock levels directly affect profit.
gui.guide_book.commerce.left_3: 3. Grouping commercial buildings together makes later logistics access and management easier.
gui.guide_book.commerce.right_1: 1. Prepare basic materials and nearby containers before opening a shop to avoid downtime.
gui.guide_book.commerce.right_2: 2. Profit drops when stock runs low, so check inventory and employee status often.
gui.guide_book.commerce.right_3: 3. Planning commerce together with logistics creates a steadier money cycle.
gui.guide_book.logistics.left_1: 1. The logistics server is the brain of the whole network, so it should usually be placed first.
gui.guide_book.logistics.left_2: 2. Logistics clients connect containers and terminals to the network and handle actual transport.
gui.guide_book.logistics.left_3: 3. A clean node layout reduces detours and makes later troubleshooting easier.
gui.guide_book.logistics.right_1: 1. Build the server first, then add clients gradually to keep the structure clear.
gui.guide_book.logistics.right_2: 2. Once warehouses, commerce and farmland are linked, item turnover becomes much more efficient.
gui.guide_book.logistics.right_3: 3. The tidier your nodes are, the easier the network is to maintain and debug.
